content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Both amendments describe real defects in follow-up routing and reconciliation. The restart path currently collapses `propose-new-bead` into the normal amendment queue when any `fix-now` item is accepted in the same round, and the propose-new-bead duplicate safeguard can incorrectly suppress an accepted follow-up based on title-only matches against closed/self beads.

## Votes

- `fr-1-7a875718`: Accept
  `restart_required` is computed from `classification.triggers_restart()`, but the restart commit path later partitions accepted amendments only by `mapped_to_bead_id`. Because `ProposeNewBead` carries no mapped target, it falls into `regular_amendments` and is written to the remediation queue whenever the same round also contains a `FixNow` item. That loses the deferred semantics and causes downstream bead-creation work to be treated as immediate restart remediation.
- `fr-1-aceccf36`: Accept
  `handle_propose_new_bead` reclassifies on the first same-title result from `br list --all` without excluding the active bead or terminal matches. This code runs during success reconciliation after the active bead has already been closed, so a title collision with the just-closed active bead or unrelated historical work can convert an accepted `ProposeNewBead` into `planned-elsewhere` and suppress creation of the intended follow-up bead.
